notes: All 6 steps completed in a single iteration. 43 new tests added (30 payload-type guards + 8 field validation + 5 tool_input normalization). All 67 tests pass.

summary: Write failing tests: null/undefined/non-object payloads silently dropped for all 6 payload-bearing handlers. For each of claude-text-delta, claude-thinking-delta, claude-tool-call, claude-tool-result, claude-done, claude-error: simulate event with payload=null, payload=undefined, payload='string', and payload=42. Assert handler() is never called. Group under a new describe('payload validation guards') block in claudeBridge.test.ts.; Add a private isRecord() helper to bridge.ts: `function isRecord(v: unknown): v is Record<string, unknown> { return v != null && typeof v === 'object' && !Array.isArray(v); }`. Add `if (!isRecord(event.payload)) return;` as the first line in all 6 payload-bearing handler callbacks. This makes step-1 tests pass while preserving all existing happy-path behavior.; Write failing tests: missing or wrong-type required string fields → handler not called. Test cases: (a) text_delta with id=123 (number instead of string), (b) text_delta with missing content field, (c) thinking_delta with content=null, (d) tool_call with tool_name missing, (e) tool_result with tool_name=42, (f) done with id=undefined, (g) error with message=[] (array instead of string), (h) error with missing id. Each should call handler 0 times.; Refactor the 6 handler callbacks to validate required string fields before constructing the OutboundMessage. For each handler: destructure from event.payload, check `typeof field !== 'string'` for each required string field, return early if any check fails. Handlers: text_delta needs id+content, thinking_delta needs id+content, tool_call needs id+tool_name, tool_result needs id+tool_name, done needs id, error needs id+message. Add console.warn on validation failure for debuggability (following meshManager pattern).; Write failing tests for tool_input normalization in tool_call handler: (a) tool_input=null → handler called with tool_input={}, (b) tool_input=[1,2,3] (array) → handler called with tool_input={}, (c) tool_input=undefined → handler called with tool_input={}, (d) tool_input='string' → handler called with tool_input={}. Verify that valid tool_input={path:'/f'} still passes through unchanged (existing test covers this but add explicit assertion).; In the tool_call handler, after field validation, normalize tool_input: `const tool_input = isRecord(p.tool_input) ? (p.tool_input as Record<string, unknown>) : {};`. This coerces null, undefined, arrays, and primitives to an empty object. Run full test suite to verify all existing happy-path tests plus new validation tests pass.
Debug fix for: Failures: tests failed, lint issues, type errors

[review-fix: merge_ff_failed] Rebase worktree branch onto latest origin/main. Fetch origin, rebase task/569 onto origin/main, resolve any conflicts (the task's changes are in gui/src/bridge.ts and gui/src/__tests__/claudeBridge.test.ts — conflicts are unlikely since main advanced in unrelated areas). Verify tests still pass after rebase.; [review-fix: portability_failure] In scripts/tree-sitter-generate.sh, replace the GNU-only `sha256sum` call on line 34 with a portable hash computation. Use `command -v sha256sum` to detect GNU coreutils, fall back to `shasum -a 256` for macOS, and exit with a clear error if neither is available. The hash function should be defined once (e.g. `compute_sha256()`) and called at line 34 for GRAMMAR_HASH. Both paths produce the same output format: hex hash followed by filename, so the `awk '{print $1}'` extraction remains unchanged.; [review-fix: race_condition] In scripts/tree-sitter-generate.sh, wrap the generation block (staleness re-check through stamp write, roughly lines 39-79) with flock-based advisory locking. Open a file descriptor on `$TS_DIR/src/.generate.lock`, acquire an exclusive lock via `flock -x`, then re-evaluate the staleness check inside the lock (to handle the lost-update case where another process already regenerated while we waited). Release happens automatically when the subshell/script exits. This prevents concurrent builds from corrupting parser.c/grammar.json/node-types.json via interleaved writes.

Post-escalation resume: fixed missing afterEach import in claudeBridge.test.ts (ReferenceError). All 69 tests pass, TypeScript type-check clean, shell script syntax valid. All 9 plan steps remain done.

Step 10: Fixed stat-failure bypass in stale-lock detection — replaced `|| echo 0` with empty sentinel, guarded age check so lock is never removed when mtime is unknown. Step 11: Branch already rebased on latest origin/main (no-op). All 937 tests pass. All 11 plan steps complete.

[review-fix: lock_bypass_on_stat_failure] In scripts/tree-sitter-generate.sh line 90, fix the stale-lock age computation so that stat failure does not unconditionally trigger lock removal. Currently `|| echo 0` produces epoch-0 when both `stat -c %Y` and `stat -f %m` fail, making `_lock_age` always >> 120 and removing active locks on unsupported platforms. Fix: (1) Compute `_lock_mtime` separately: `_lock_mtime=$(stat -c %Y "$LOCK_DIR" 2>/dev/null || stat -f %m "$LOCK_DIR" 2>/dev/null || echo '')`. (2) Guard the age check: `if [ -n "$_lock_mtime" ]; then _lock_age=$(( $(date +%s) - _lock_mtime )); if [ "$_lock_age" -gt 120 ]; then ... rmdir ... continue; fi; fi`. (3) If `_lock_mtime` is empty (stat failed), fall through to the existing hard-failure `exit 1` — the lock cannot be verified as stale, so we must not remove it.; [review-fix: merge_ff_failed] Rebase worktree branch onto latest origin/main (main advanced again since last rebase in step-7). Fetch origin, rebase task/569 onto origin/main, resolve any conflicts. The task's changes span gui/src/bridge.ts, gui/src/__tests__/claudeBridge.test.ts, and scripts/tree-sitter-generate.sh — conflicts are unlikely since main typically advances in unrelated areas. Verify tests still pass after rebase.

[review-fix: portability_failure in test script] In scripts/test_tree_sitter_generate.sh, replace the two direct `sha256sum` calls on lines 51 and 111 with a portable hash helper. Define a `compute_hash()` function at the top of the test script (after variable declarations, before Test 1) that mirrors the generate script's `compute_sha256()` logic: `compute_hash() { if command -v sha256sum >/dev/null 2>&1; then sha256sum "$1"; elif command -v shasum >/dev/null 2>&1; then shasum -a 256 "$1"; else echo "ERROR: neither sha256sum nor shasum found" >&2; exit 1; fi; }`. Then replace line 51 `expected_hash=$(sha256sum "$TS_DIR/grammar.js" | awk '{print $1}')` with `expected_hash=$(compute_hash "$TS_DIR/grammar.js" | awk '{print $1}')`, and replace line 111 `real_hash=$(sha256sum "$TS_DIR/grammar.js" | awk '{print $1}')` with `real_hash=$(compute_hash "$TS_DIR/grammar.js" | awk '{print $1}')`. This ensures the test script works on macOS (where only `shasum -a 256` is available) and doesn't abort with 'command not found' under `set -euo pipefail`.; [review-fix: merge_ff_failed] Rebase worktree branch onto latest origin/main (main advanced again since last rebase in step-11). Fetch origin, rebase task/569 onto origin/main, resolve any conflicts.
